terms.definition: A system-level measure, borrowed from thermodynamics, that quantifies the dispersion of moral, cognitive, and institutional order within a society (higher values = greater disorder).
An abrupt shift from one macroscopic order state to another once hidden variables cross a critical threshold; here, the transition from cohesive to fractured moral consensus.
The progressive erosion of stable, shared meanings assigned to core moral or civic vocabulary, resulting in communicative decoherence.
Loss of phase alignment among informational carriers, leading to breakdown of coherent signal transmission; metaphorically applied to public discourse.
A five-pillar analytic lens: Family, Authority, Cognition, Transcendence, and Social-trust metrics, used to track civilizational entropy.
